Details: Inactivation of ricin in water by ultraviolet (UV) irradiation was found to be effective by one study. Treated ricin was injected subcutaneously to mice. One to five minutes of contact time were sufficient to destroy toxicity so that two to three times the lethal doses of treated ricin could be injected into mice without causing the same symptoms observed with untreated ricin.  This suggests UV could be effective under drinking water conditions.
